Entry.heading: Modular Consoles – Migration Required
Entry.long_description:
[h1]The original Modular Consoles Mod is no longer maintained[/h1]
Tom_is_unlucky, the original developer of [cyan]Modular Consoles[/cyan], has been unreachable for some time. I don't know the details, and I wish him well - but in his absence, the original mod is incompatible with the current game version and there is no way for me to push fixes to Tom's Workshop.
I have taken over maintenance of the mod under my own Workshop page. The new version is up to date, actively maintained, and [green] a drop-in replacement for the original.[/green]
Clicking [yellow]Migrate[/yellow] below will automatically unsubscribe you from the old version and subscribe you to the maintained version on my (WIKUS's) workshop. [green]Your saves are safe.[/green]
[yellow] Note: [/yellow] [yellow] If you want to keep using the old version because you play on an older release branch, [/yellow] [yellow] click ignore below instead. [/yellow]
~ [b]Wikus with a little help from JacksonTheMaster, Aproposmath and Inaki[/b]

Entry.heading: LibConstruct – Migration Required
Entry.long_description:
[h1]The original LibConstruct Mod is no longer maintained[/h1]
But wait - what even is LibConstruct? [cyan]LibConstruct[/cyan] is a shared library that [cyan]Modular Consoles[/cyan] and some other mods depends on. It was authored by Tom_is_unlucky, who has been unreachable for some time. We don't know the details, and we wish him well - but in his absence, the mod has broken on the current game version and there is no way for us to push fixes to Toms Workshop.
Just like Modular Consoles, we've published a maintained version under WIKUS's Workshop page. Clicking [yellow]Migrate[/yellow] below will unsubscribe you from the old Mod and subscribe you to the working one automatically.
[yellow] Note: [/yellow] [yellow] If you want to keep using the old version because you play on an older release branch, [/yellow] [yellow] click ignore below instead. [/yellow]
~ [b]Wikus with a little help from JacksonTheMaster, Aproposmath and Inaki[/b]
